chunks.text: Course: CS 169L | EECS at UC Berkeley CS 169L. Software Engineering Team Project Catalog Description: Open-ended design project enhancing or creating software for real customers in an agile team setting. Teamwork coordination, effective customer meetings, pre- and post-iteration team meetings, running scrums and standups, technical communication. Contributing as a team to an open-source project; tools and workflows associated with open source collaboration, including fork-and-pull, rebase, upstream merge, continuous deployment & integration. Units: 4 Also Offered As: COMPSCI 169L Course Objectives: Students will work in a team to develop new software or enhance existing software for a customer with a real business need. Student Learning Outcomes: Students will learn to run a small team including rotation of team roles such as product owner, scrum master, and so on; Students will learn how to conduct effective meetings with nontechnical customers and work with their feedback; Students will learn how to coordinate teamwork on developing, testing, and deploying features; and in most cases, how to approach a legacy codebase and add features to it. Prerequisites: COMPSCI 169A or COMPSCI W169A Formats: Summer: 6.0 hours of discussion and 16.0 hours of fieldwork per week Spring: 3.0 hours of discussion and 8.0 hours of fieldwork per week Fall: 3.0 hours of discussion and 8.0 hours of fieldwork per week Grading Basis: Student Option Final Exam Status: Alternate Method Class Schedule (Spring 2026): CS 169L – MoFr 10:30-11:59, Soda 320 – Armando Fox , Michael Ball Class homepage on bCourses Class Notes - Time conflicts
